skill.name: denario
skill.description: Denario guides AI assistants through multiagent scientific research workflows. It covers dataset description, hypothesis generation, methodology planning, computational analysis, and LaTeX paper creation.
skill.summary: Automates scientific research workflows from data context to publication-ready manuscripts.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, network, and credential patterns. Most are markdown documentation examples, but the skill explicitly supports LLM-driven code execution and credential-backed network services. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found, so this is high risk rather than blocked.

high_findings.title: Automated LLM-Driven Code Execution
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: Denario documentation states that result generation executes methodology code and can automatically write and execute code. This is intended functionality, but it is dangerous with untrusted prompts, datasets, or generated methods.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly describes agent execution of analysis code. Legitimate research use is possible, but the execution surface is explicit and high impact.

high_findings.title: Credential and Secret Handling in Workflows
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to configure API keys, service account JSON files, .env files, and Docker credential mounts. These are legitimate setup steps, but mistakes can expose sensitive credentials.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple cited sections handle real cloud and LLM credentials. The same file also gives security guidance, so this is risky configuration rather than confirmed theft.

high_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The documentation recommends a curl command piped directly into bash for Google Cloud SDK installation. This is a known risky installation pattern because remote content runs without local review.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell command is present in a setup section. It appears copied from vendor installation guidance, but the execution risk is clear.

high_findings.title: Privileged System Package Installation
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The installation guide includes a sudo apt-get command for a full TeX installation. This is common documentation, but it requires elevated privileges and installs a large dependency set.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and privileged. The context is normal LaTeX setup, so the confidence is high for risk but not for malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Package and Docker Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill directs users to install Denario from package tooling, clone a public repository, and run a Docker image. These commands are expected for this tool, but they expand the trusted supply chain.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are documented setup steps with no hidden obfuscation. The risk comes from executing external packages and containers from a community source.

medium_findings.title: Networked LLM and Literature Services
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill depends on external LLM providers and describes literature-search integration. Network access is core functionality, but it can expose prompts, dataset summaries, and research context to third parties.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The provider and literature-search references are explicit. No evidence shows unauthorized network exfiltration, so this remains a contextual risk.

medium_findings.title: Project Filesystem Writes
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: Examples create project directories, save workflow files, and describe generated outputs such as figures, LaTeX, PDFs, and logs. This is normal workflow behavior but should be scoped to a dedicated project directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are direct and expected. The evidence does not show attempts to read unrelated local files or modify hidden system locations.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Keywords Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static weak-cryptography, ransomware, and reconnaissance detections mostly match scientific terms, shell examples, or security best-practice text. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, ransomware behavior, or active reconnaissance logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts are descriptions and documentation, not executable malicious code. The broad static labels do not match the surrounding semantics.

low_findings.title: Promotional Behavior Steering
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The skill asks the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not a direct security exploit, but it is marketplace-relevant behavioral steering toward an external hosted service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is visible and specific. It does not override safety rules or skip review, but it can influence assistant recommendations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe to Shell Setup Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes curl output piped into bash. Users should avoid this pattern or inspect installation scripts before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present and semantically risky.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Files and Environment Variables
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses API keys, .env files, service account JSON files, and Docker environment injection. Users must keep these secrets out of prompts, repositories, and logs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence repeatedly references real secret storage and propagation mechanisms.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Code Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The result stage can execute code produced from generated methodologies. This should be sandboxed and reviewed before running.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly states code execution during analysis generation.

content.value_statement: Scientific research workflows require repeated planning, analysis, writing, and revision. Denario helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code coordinate those stages with structured research artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Guides dataset description and research context setup.
Generates research ideas from described data and available tools.
Develops methodology documents for computational experiments.
Runs or supports result generation through Denario project workflows.
Creates LaTeX manuscript outputs with journal-oriented formatting.
Documents installation, Docker usage, and LLM provider configuration.
limitations: Requires external Denario installation and compatible Python dependencies.
Requires user-provided LLM credentials for supported providers.
Generated methods and results require expert scientific review.
Automated execution can run code and should be isolated from sensitive systems.

use_cases.title: Explore a New Dataset
use_cases.description: Turn a dataset description into research questions, methods, and expected analyses.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Reproducible Study
use_cases.description: Create structured project files, figures, results notes, and manuscript drafts.

use_cases.title: Draft a Journal Manuscript
use_cases.description: Convert completed methods and results into a formatted LaTeX paper draft.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Research Project
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new dataset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Denario to help me define a research project. My dataset is [describe data]. My available tools are [list tools]. Suggest clear research directions.

prompt_templates.title: Develop a Methodology
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning after a research question exists.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Denario to develop a methodology for this research idea: [idea]. Include data preparation, analysis methods, validation steps, and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Pipeline Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Denario to plan an end-to-end workflow for [project]. Before execution, list files that will be written, commands required, credentials needed, and review checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Paper Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced manuscript creation from existing results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Denario to prepare a journal-style manuscript from these results: [summarize results]. Include paper structure, figure placement, limitations, and citation needs.

output_examples.input: A climate researcher describes global temperature anomaly data and asks for a research plan.
output: A focused hypothesis about warming trends and acceleration.
A methodology covering preprocessing, trend analysis, validation, and visualization.
A manuscript outline with expected figures and result sections.

output_examples.input: A student provides completed traffic optimization results and asks for a paper draft plan.
output: A paper structure with abstract, methods, results, discussion, and limitations.
Recommendations for figure placement and statistical reporting.
A checklist for expert review before submission.

output_examples.input: A research engineer asks how to run Denario with OpenAI or Vertex AI credentials.
output: A configuration checklist for environment variables and credential files.
Warnings about secret storage, Docker mounts, and version control exclusions.
A recommendation to test in an isolated project directory.

best_practices: Run generated analyses in an isolated environment with limited file and network access.
Review every generated methodology before allowing result execution.
Keep API keys and credential files out of repositories and shared logs.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated code on production machines or sensitive datasets without review.
Do not paste real API keys into prompts, notebooks, or shared project files.
Do not treat generated scientific conclusions as validated evidence without expert review.

faq.question: What does Denario help automate?
faq.answer: It helps automate research idea generation, methodology planning, computational analysis workflows, and LaTeX paper drafting.

faq.question: Does this skill require external software?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation expects Denario, Python 3.12 or higher, and optional Docker or LaTeX tooling.

faq.question: Does it need API keys?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes configuration for LLM providers such as OpenAI and Google Vertex AI.

faq.question: Can it execute code?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation states that generated result workflows can write and execute analysis code.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Run it in isolated environments and avoid exposing secrets, regulated data, or private datasets.

faq.question: Should generated papers be submitted directly?
faq.answer: No. Experts should review the methods, data handling, results, citations, and conclusions before submission.
